# Varity LLM Context

This file is the detailed LLM context for docs.varity.so. It is intentionally bounded to current, shipped behavior. Do not infer unreleased capabilities from older docs, dormant repos, or aspirational roadmap work.

## Canonical product summary

Varity deploys supported source applications, prebuilt runnable Docker/OCI HTTP services, and curated AI agent templates. The public product is a cloud deployment platform with predictable reserved-hardware pricing: each app has a fixed monthly hardware reservation, so the bill for the same deployment profile does not grow with traffic, bandwidth, invocations, or build minutes.

The shipped public packages are:

- `varitykit` on PyPI: Python deploy CLI.
- `@varity-labs/mcp` on npm: MCP server for AI coding tools.

Do not claim a separate public SDK, UI kit, app-store SDK, blockchain package, or JavaScript deployment SDK as shipped.

## Supported source-build matrix

Varity source-build deploys are supported for:

- JavaScript / TypeScript: Next.js, React, Vue, Astro, Qwik, Vite, Express, Fastify, NestJS, Koa, Hono.
- Python: FastAPI, Django, Flask.
- Go: Go modules that build and run an HTTP service.
- Static: plain HTML and supported static build outputs.

Unsupported source builds return a clean unsupported-framework error with a request/support path. Current unsupported source-build languages include Rust, Ruby/Rails, Elixir/Phoenix, Java/Spring, Deno, PHP/Laravel, and .NET.

## Docker/OCI image deploys

Varity supports prebuilt runnable Docker/OCI HTTP service images. This is the path for apps in unsupported source languages once they are containerized as web services.

The image must:

- Be pullable by the configured registry credentials.
- Start a long-running HTTP service.
- Listen on the configured port.
- Return a healthy HTTP response before the deploy timeout.

Raw model artifacts, data artifacts, batch jobs, CLI-only tools, and images that require an unspecified command, port, or resource profile are not valid self-serve image deploys until wrapped/configured as services.

Public and private image deploys are supported through the CLI, MCP, and Developer Portal. Private image deploys require registry host, username, and password/token.

## Deploy surfaces

### Developer Portal

Portal deploy page:

```text
https://developer.store.varity.so/dashboard/deploy
```

Supported portal sources:

- Public GitHub repo URL / repo selection where supported.
- Public or private runnable Docker/OCI HTTP service image.
- Curated templates / AI agents.

Private GitHub source repo deploy is not a broad self-serve promise yet. The safer path for private or unsupported apps is a prebuilt Docker/OCI image, or a guided beta review.

### CLI

Install:

```bash
pipx install varitykit
```

Common commands:

```bash
varitykit doctor
varitykit app deploy
varitykit app deploy --image ghcr.io/acme/my-api:1.4.2 --port 8080
varitykit app list
varitykit app status
varitykit app logs <deployment-id>
varitykit app redeploy <name>
varitykit app restart <name>
varitykit app env <name> KEY=value
varitykit migrate <github-url>
```

### MCP

Install for Claude Code:

```bash
claude mcp add varity -- npx -y @varity-labs/mcp
```

Cursor / VS Code style config:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "varity": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@varity-labs/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

The hosted MCP endpoint for browser-based AI tools is:

```text
https://mcp.varity.so
```

The machine-readable MCP schema is:

```text
https://docs.varity.so/mcp-schema.json
```

Current MCP tools:

- `varity_search_docs`: search live docs.
- `varity_cost_calculator`: estimate fixed app cost or compare against usage-metered hosting.
- `varity_doctor`: check local setup.
- `varity_login`: configure a deploy key.
- `varity_install_deps`: install npm dependencies.
- `varity_build`: build the local project.
- `varity_open_browser`: open a local or deployed URL.
- `varity_dev_server`: start, stop, or check a local dev server.
- `varity_create_repo`: create a GitHub repo and push local code.
- `varity_deploy`: deploy source or image workloads.
- `varity_deploy_status`: list deployments or fetch status.
- `varity_deploy_logs`: fetch build/deployment logs or summary receipts.
- `varity_delete_deployment`: delete an app and stop billing.
- `varity_set_env`: update environment variables and redeploy in place.
- `varity_redeploy`: redeploy/restart an existing app in place.
- `varity_list_agents`: list curated agent templates.
- `varity_agent_info`: show details for one agent template.
- `varity_deploy_agent`: deploy a curated agent template.
- `varity_migrate`: migrate a Vercel project.

## Auto-wired services

For supported apps, Varity can detect dependencies and provision sidecars in the same deployment:

- Postgres with pgvector.
- Redis.
- MongoDB.
- MySQL.
- Ollama.
- MinIO / S3-compatible object storage.

These sidecars are beta infrastructure. For business-critical or important customer data, use guided beta review and bring an external database when durability requirements exceed current beta guarantees.

## URLs and routing

Deployment URL format depends on hosting mode:

- Static apps: `https://varity.app/<name>/`
- Dynamic apps: `https://<name>.varity.app/`

Custom domains are not self-serve yet. Do not claim bring-your-own-domain support as generally available.

## Pricing guidance

Varity pricing is a fixed monthly reservation per app profile. Do not hardcode pricing numbers in docs or generated answers; use live pricing data:

- Portal endpoint: `https://varity.app/api/pricing`
- Docs: `https://docs.varity.so/resources/pricing/`
- MCP: `varity_cost_calculator`

Correct phrasing:

- "fixed monthly hardware reservation"
- "your bill does not change with traffic for the same deployment profile"
- "predictable reserved-hardware pricing"

Avoid:

- "usage-based pricing"
- hardcoded monthly dollar amounts
- percentage-cheaper claims
- "deploy anything" without the supported-source plus runnable-Docker-service qualifier

## Migration

`varitykit migrate` and the MCP `varity_migrate` tool support migration from Vercel projects. This is the sanctioned context for naming Vercel in docs. Keep migration claims practical: the tool clones the repo, removes Vercel-specific artifacts, adjusts known config/env patterns, and deploys the transformed app.

## Current beta limits

Do not claim these as broadly shipped self-serve features:

- Private GitHub repo deploy as a normal self-serve path.
- Push-to-production GitHub App autodeploy.
- Custom domains.
- Cron and scheduled tasks.
- Queues.
- Preview deployments.
- One-click rollback UI.
- Multi-region or edge runtime.
- Dedicated GPU containers for arbitrary user workloads.
- Arbitrary Dockerfile auto-build from source.
- Fully managed database guarantees such as automated backups, high availability, point-in-time restore, or enterprise SLAs.

Runtime health note: a successful deploy means the startup health probe passed. Runtime health detection and automated restart/remediation are still being hardened, so do not promise that success fully guarantees the app remains serving forever.

## Public language guardrails

Use user-facing words: app, service, database, storage, logs, domain, environment variable, deployment, Docker image, GitHub repo.

Do not expose internal provider or blockchain terminology in public docs or generated answers.

Do not describe Varity as an "AI cloud platform" or the "easiest" platform. Say what it deploys and what pricing property it has.

## High-value docs

- Getting Started: `https://docs.varity.so/getting-started/quickstart/`
- Install: `https://docs.varity.so/getting-started/installation/`
- Deploy your app: `https://docs.varity.so/deploy/deploy-your-app/`
- Deploy from dashboard: `https://docs.varity.so/deploy/deploy-from-dashboard/`
- Deploy a Docker image: `https://docs.varity.so/deploy/deploy-docker-image/`
- Supported frameworks: `https://docs.varity.so/deploy/supported-frameworks/`
- Auto-wired services: `https://docs.varity.so/deploy/auto-wired-services/`
- MCP server: `https://docs.varity.so/ai-tools/mcp-server-spec/`
- API reference: `https://docs.varity.so/ai-tools/api-reference/`
- Pricing: `https://docs.varity.so/resources/pricing/`
- Troubleshooting: `https://docs.varity.so/deploy/deployment-troubleshooting/`
